title: Find if Path Exists in Graph
hint: Traverse from source and check whether destination becomes reachable.
intuition: Connected nodes become reachable through graph traversal.

title: Number of Connected Components in an Undirected Graph
hint: Run DFS/BFS from every unvisited node.
intuition: Each traversal fully covers one component.

title: Clone Graph
hint: Use hashmap to map original nodes to cloned nodes.
intuition: Graphs require preserving already-created node references.

title: Keys and Rooms
hint: Traverse rooms using collected keys.
intuition: Keys form directed graph edges between rooms.

title: All Paths From Source to Target
hint: Backtrack all possible paths recursively.
intuition: DFS naturally explores complete path possibilities.

title: Find Center of Star Graph
hint: The center appears in every edge.
intuition: Star graphs have one node connected to all others.

title: Find the Town Judge
hint: Judge trusts nobody but everyone trusts the judge.
intuition: Indegree and outdegree fully define judge conditions.

title: Network Delay Time
hint: Use min heap to always process closest node first.
intuition: Shortest known path should expand first.

title: Possible Bipartition
hint: Adjacent nodes must receive opposite colors.
intuition: Bipartite graphs can be separated into two groups.

title: Is Graph Bipartite?
hint: Color neighboring nodes with opposite colors.
intuition: Odd-length cycles break bipartite structure.

title: Reorder Routes to Make All Paths Lead to the City Zero
hint: Count edges pointing away from city zero during traversal.
intuition: Traversal reveals incorrectly directed roads.

title: Min Cost to Connect All Points
hint: Always connect the cheapest unvisited point.
intuition: MST grows optimally one edge at a time.

title: Number of Islands
hint: Start DFS/BFS whenever you find an unvisited land cell.
intuition: Each traversal fully covers one island component.

title: Max Area of Island
hint: Count cells while exploring each island.
intuition: DFS naturally measures connected region size.

title: Flood Fill
hint: Expand only into cells matching the original color.
intuition: Flood fill spreads through connected equal-valued cells.

title: 01 Matrix
hint: Start BFS from all zero cells simultaneously.
intuition: Multiple BFS sources compute nearest distances efficiently.
commonMistakes: Running BFS from every cell separately

title: Rotting Oranges
hint: All rotten oranges become BFS starting points.
intuition: Each BFS level represents one minute of spread.

title: Surrounded Regions
hint: Mark regions connected to boundaries first.
intuition: Boundary-connected regions cannot be captured.

title: Walls and Gates
hint: Push all gates into queue initially.
intuition: Nearest gate distance spreads outward layer by layer.
commonMistakes: Running BFS separately for every room

title: Pacific Atlantic Water Flow
hint: Traverse inward from oceans instead of outward from cells.
intuition: Reverse traversal dramatically reduces repeated searches.

title: Shortest Path in Binary Matrix
hint: Use BFS because every move has equal cost.
intuition: BFS guarantees shortest path in unweighted grids.

title: As Far from Land as Possible
hint: Expand outward from all land cells simultaneously.
intuition: The last reached water cell becomes the farthest.

title: Nearest Exit from Entrance in Maze
hint: First exit reached in BFS is the shortest path.
intuition: BFS explores all shortest-distance cells first.

title: Word Search
hint: Backtrack while marking cells as visited temporarily.
intuition: Each path choice affects future traversal possibilities.

title: Course Schedule
hint: Use recursion stack tracking to detect cycles.
intuition: A cycle makes course completion impossible.
commonMistakes: Using only visited array without path tracking

title: Course Schedule II
hint: Generate valid order using indegree-based BFS.
intuition: Nodes with zero indegree are immediately solvable.
commonMistakes: Returning partial order when cycle exists

title: Redundant Connection
hint: If two nodes already share the same parent, cycle exists.
intuition: Extra edge connecting same component forms a cycle.

title: Redundant Connection II
hint: Handle both cycle case and double-parent case.
intuition: Directed graphs introduce parent conflicts beyond normal cycles.

title: Detect Cycles in 2D Grid
hint: Track previous cell to avoid false cycle detection.
intuition: Returning to an already visited valid cell forms a cycle.

title: Longest Cycle in a Graph
hint: Track discovery step index during traversal.
intuition: Cycle length equals repeated node distance difference.

title: Find Eventual Safe States
hint: Safe nodes never reach a cycle.
intuition: Cycle participation determines unsafe states.

title: Alien Dictionary
hint: Build character ordering graph from adjacent words.
intuition: Character precedence constraints form a DAG.

title: Parallel Courses
hint: Each BFS level represents one semester.
intuition: Courses with zero indegree can be taken together.

title: Parallel Courses III
hint: Track maximum completion time for each course.
intuition: Each course depends on the slowest prerequisite chain.

title: Sequence Reconstruction
hint: Topological order must always have exactly one valid next node.
intuition: Multiple zero-indegree choices imply multiple valid orders.

title: Minimum Height Trees
hint: Repeatedly remove leaf nodes level by level.
intuition: Tree centers remain after trimming outer layers.

title: Sort Items by Groups Respecting Dependencies
hint: Perform topological sort on both groups and items.
intuition: Groups create another dependency layer above items.
commonMistakes: Mixing item graph with group graph

title: Build a Matrix With Conditions
hint: Compute row order and column order separately.
intuition: Independent constraints can be solved independently.
commonMistakes: Using one ordering for both dimensions

title: Loud and Rich
hint: Propagate quietest richer person through graph edges.
intuition: Topological order guarantees prerequisites are processed first.

title: Largest Color Value in a Directed Graph
hint: Maintain color frequencies while processing topological order.
intuition: DAG processing allows incremental path aggregation.

title: Number of Provinces
hint: Union directly connected cities together.
intuition: Connected nodes belong to the same component group.

title: Accounts Merge
hint: Union accounts sharing the same email.
intuition: Shared identifiers connect related accounts.

title: Graph Valid Tree
hint: A valid tree must have n-1 edges and no cycles.
intuition: Trees are connected acyclic graphs.

title: Smallest String With Swaps
hint: Indices in same component can be rearranged freely.
intuition: Connected positions share swap accessibility.

title: Satisfiability of Equality Equations
hint: Union equal variables first, then validate inequalities.
intuition: Equality constraints form connected groups.

title: Most Stones Removed with Same Row or Column
hint: Stones sharing row or column belong to same component.
intuition: One stone per connected component must remain.

title: Lexicographically Smallest Equivalent String
hint: Always map component to smallest lexicographical character.
intuition: Equivalent characters belong to interchangeable groups.

title: Number of Operations to Make Network Connected
hint: Extra edges can reconnect disconnected components.
intuition: Connecting k components requires at least k-1 edges.

title: Cheapest Flights Within K Stops
hint: Track both cost and remaining stops during traversal.
intuition: Shortest cost depends on stop constraints too.

title: Path With Minimum Effort
hint: Distance becomes maximum edge difference so far.
intuition: Optimal path minimizes worst jump instead of total sum.

title: Swim in Rising Water
hint: Always expand the currently reachable minimum height cell.
intuition: Water level equals highest elevation encountered so far.

title: Minimum Cost to Make at Least One Valid Path in a Grid
hint: Use deque: zero-cost moves go front, cost-one moves go back.
intuition: 0-1 BFS optimizes graphs with only two edge weights.

title: The Maze II
hint: Ball keeps rolling until hitting a wall.
intuition: Stopping positions become graph nodes.

title: Reachable Nodes In Subdivided Graph
hint: Compute shortest distance to every original node first.
intuition: Remaining moves determine subdivided node reachability.

title: Shortest Path Visiting All Nodes
hint: State = current node + visited mask.
intuition: Different visited states create different traversal possibilities.

title: Minimum Genetic Mutation
hint: Every valid mutation becomes a graph edge.
intuition: BFS guarantees minimum transformation count.

title: Word Ladder
hint: Each one-letter transformation creates graph connectivity.
intuition: BFS finds minimum transformation steps naturally.

title: Path with Maximum Probability
hint: Use max heap based on probability instead of distance.
intuition: Highest probability path behaves like reversed shortest path.

title: Connecting Cities With Minimum Cost
hint: Sort edges by weight and greedily connect components.
intuition: Cheapest non-cycling edges build the MST optimally.

title: Optimize Water Distribution in a Village
hint: Create a virtual node representing building wells.
intuition: Well construction behaves like connecting to a source node.

title: Min Cost to Connect All Points
hint: Always connect the nearest unvisited point.
intuition: MST grows optimally from already connected nodes.

title: Find Critical and Pseudo-Critical Edges in Minimum Spanning Tree
hint: Compare MST cost while forcing or skipping edges.
intuition: Critical edges are necessary for optimal MST cost.

title: Critical Connections in a Network
hint: Track discovery time and lowest reachable ancestor.
intuition: A bridge disconnects the graph if removed.

title: Reconstruct Itinerary
hint: Use Hierholzer’s algorithm with lexical ordering.
intuition: Every ticket edge must be used exactly once.

title: Evaluate Division
hint: Treat variables as graph nodes with weighted edges.
intuition: Path multiplication produces final ratio.

title: Open the Lock
hint: Every wheel rotation forms a neighboring state.
intuition: BFS guarantees minimum moves between states.

title: Snakes and Ladders
hint: Each dice throw generates up to six next states.
intuition: Board traversal behaves like shortest path search.

title: Bus Routes
hint: Treat bus routes as graph transitions.
intuition: Changing buses forms traversal edges.

title: Shortest Bridge
hint: Mark one island first, then BFS outward.
intuition: First island expansion reaches shortest bridge earliest.

title: Minimum Knight Moves
hint: Knight positions form graph states.
intuition: BFS guarantees minimum number of moves.

title: Remove Max Number of Edges to Keep Graph Fully Traversable
hint: Use shared edges before exclusive edges.
intuition: Shared edges maximize connectivity efficiency.

title: Detonate the Maximum Bombs
hint: Create directed edges based on bomb ranges.
intuition: Bomb explosions propagate through reachable nodes.

title: Freedom Trail
hint: State depends on current ring position and key index.
intuition: Each rotation creates transition costs between states.

title: Shortest Path to Get All Keys
hint: State = position + collected keys bitmask.
intuition: Different key combinations create different reachable worlds.
